description_long: This is a tattered, vertically hanging banner with a deep maroon fabric, featuring a large black fleur-de-lis symbol at its center. The banner is heavily weathered, with significant tears and missing sections along the bottom and sides, giving it a distressed, aged appearance. It is suspended from a thin, horizontal metal rod at the top, with additional rods or supports visible at intervals along its length, likely to keep it taut. The fabric appears to be thick and heavy, possibly canvas or a similar durable textile, and the edges are frayed. The support structure is minimal and metallic, with small brackets or hooks at the ends. The overall look suggests it is intended for outdoor use, possibly as a decorative or heraldic element in a historical or themed urban setting.
description: A distressed maroon banner with a black fleur-de-lis, torn and frayed, hanging from a metal rod with visible support brackets.
description_view_0: A side profile showing the thinness of the banner and the linear arrangement of the metal supports.
description_view_1: A frontal view displaying the maroon banner with a black fleur-de-lis, extensive tears, and frayed edges.
description_view_2: The opposite side profile, again highlighting the slim structure and the staggered placement of support rods.
description_view_3: A rear or alternate frontal view, showing the reverse side of the banner with similar tears and the fleur-de-lis partially visible.
